title: Range Phrase Reference
description: Range elements are filtering syntax used after the 'range' keyword in PFC commands. They select subsets of objects (balls, walls, contacts, etc.) based on geometric regions, attributes, or logical conditions.

examples: contact property fric 0.5 range cylinder end-1 0 0 0 end-2 0 0 10 radius 5
notes: Multiple range elements create intersection (AND) by default
Unspecified range affects all objects of target type

elements.name: position
elements.description: Bounding box selection. Two vectors define opposite corners.

elements.name: position-x
elements.description: Filter by X-coordinate range.

elements.name: position-y
elements.description: Filter by Y-coordinate range.

elements.name: position-z
elements.description: Filter by Z-coordinate range.

elements.name: sphere
elements.description: Spherical region selection.

elements.name: cylinder
elements.description: Cylindrical region. Two radii create annular (hollow) cylinder.

elements.name: annulus
elements.description: Spherical annulus (hollow sphere) selection.

elements.name: plane
elements.description: Infinite plane selection. Use above/below or distance from plane.

elements.name: polygon
elements.description: Polygonal cylinder extending perpendicular to polygon plane. Optional origin creates truncated cone.

elements.name: rectangle
elements.description: Rectangular cylinder. Four vertices define the rectangle.

elements.name: ellipse
elements.description: Ellipse inscribed in rectangle defined by four vertices.

categories.name: Attribute Elements
categories.description: Select objects by ID, group, or other attributes

elements.name: id
elements.description: Select by ID range. Single value for exact match, two values for inclusive range.

elements.name: id-list
elements.description: Select by explicit list of IDs. Non-sequential values allowed.

elements.name: group
elements.description: Select by group membership. Supports slot specification and logical combinations.

elements.name: named-range
elements.description: Reference a previously created named range (via 'model range create').

categories.name: PFC-Specific Elements
categories.description: Elements specific to PFC (balls, walls, contacts)

elements.name: contact
elements.description: Filter contacts by activity state, gap distance, contact model, or type.

parameters.name: gap
parameters.description: Gap range. Single value: <= gap; two values: f1 <= gap <= f2

categories.name: User-Defined Elements
categories.description: Custom filtering via extra variables or FISH

elements.name: extra
elements.description: Filter by extra variable value at specified index.

elements.name: extra-list
elements.description: Filter by extra variable matching integer list.

elements.name: fish
elements.description: Custom FISH function for filtering. Function receives position and object pointer, returns true/false.
notes: Not allowed in plot windows due to potential side effects

categories.name: Element Modifiers
categories.description: Modify behavior of individual range elements

elements.name: not
elements.description: Invert the element's selection (exclude instead of include).

elements.name: extent
elements.description: Require entire object extent inside region (not just centroid). Only for geometric elements.

elements.name: all
elements.description: Require ALL sub-objects to match (default: any sub-object match selects parent).

elements.name: by
elements.description: Specify object hierarchy level for evaluation.

categories.name: Phrase Modifiers
categories.description: Modify behavior of entire range phrase

elements.name: union
elements.description: Change from intersection (AND) to union (OR) logic for multiple elements.

common_patterns.name: Set property by group
common_patterns.description: Set density for balls in group

common_patterns.name: Cylindrical confining
common_patterns.example: wall attribute velocity (0.001,0,0) range cylinder end-1 0 0 0 end-2 0 0 10 radius 5
common_patterns.description: Set wall velocity in cylindrical region

common_patterns.name: Exclude boundary
common_patterns.example: ball fix velocity range group 'boundary' not
common_patterns.description: Fix all balls except boundary group

common_patterns.name: Named range reuse
common_patterns.example:
model range create 'core' cylinder end-1 0 0 0 end-2 0 0 10 radius 3
ball attribute density 2500 range named-range 'core'
